mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice risk assets.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate above 4.5% signals weakening labor markets that trigger defensive equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for 3+ months, or t-test p > 0.1 for return differential vs baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal weak aggregate demand, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when both INDPRO and HOUST fall by >1.5% YoY, or pairwise correlation between proxies and returns drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production above 103.0 signals strengthening supply-side capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings outlook.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO exceeds 103.0 for 3+ months, or IC < 0.1 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in CPI inflation below 2.5% triggers central bank policy patience, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL < 2.5%, or t-test p > 0.05 for return differential vs non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST rise >2.0% MoM, or interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.1) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200k new jobs signals strong labor market dynamics that drive S&P 500 positive returns over 60 trading days via consumer spending momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when PAYEMS exceeds 200k for 3+ months, or IC < 0.15 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2.0% signals economic contraction that drives S&P 500 negative returns over 60 trading days through recession risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when GDPC1 < 2.0%, or t-test p > 0.05 for return differential vs non-trigger periods.
